import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from datetime import datetime
from great_tables import GT
from great_tables.data_color import data_color
from great_tables.style import style
import re

# Load the data
df = pd.read_csv('sp500.csv')

# Convert date to datetime
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date'])

# Sort by date
df = df.sort_values('date').reset_index(drop=True)

# Extract year from date
df['year'] = df['date'].dt.year

# Get the start and end dates
start_date = df['date'].min()
end_date = df['date'].max()

# Calculate yearly summary statistics
yearly_stats = []

for year in sorted(df['year'].unique()):
    year_data = df[df['year'] == year]

    # Get first and last closing price of the year
    first_close = year_data.iloc[0]['close']
    last_close = year_data.iloc[-1]['close']

    # Calculate the change and percentage change
    year_change = last_close - first_close
    year_pct_change = (year_change / first_close) * 100

    # Get high and low for the year
    year_high = year_data['high'].max()
    year_low = year_data['low'].min()

    # Get opening price for the year
    opening = year_data.iloc[0]['open']

    # Get total volume for the year
    total_volume = year_data['volume'].sum()

    yearly_stats.append({
        'Year': year,
        'Opening': opening,
        'Closing': last_close,
        'High': year_high,
        'Low': year_low,
        'Change': year_change,
        'Change %': year_pct_change,
        'Avg Volume': total_volume / len(year_data),
        'Trading Days': len(year_data)
    })

# Create DataFrame from yearly stats
summary_df = pd.DataFrame(yearly_stats)

# Calculate overall 5-year statistics
overall_first_close = df.iloc[0]['close']
overall_last_close = df.iloc[-1]['close']
overall_change = overall_last_close - overall_first_close
overall_pct_change = (overall_change / overall_first_close) * 100
overall_high = df['high'].max()
overall_low = df['low'].min()

# Add a row for 5-Year Overview at the top
overview_row = pd.DataFrame([{
    'Year': '5-Year Overview',
    'Opening': overall_first_close,
    'Closing': overall_last_close,
    'High': overall_high,
    'Low': overall_low,
    'Change': overall_change,
    'Change %': overall_pct_change,
    'Avg Volume': df['volume'].mean(),
    'Trading Days': len(df)
}])

# Combine overview with yearly data
display_df = pd.concat([overview_row, summary_df], ignore_index=True)

# Format the dataframe for display
display_df['Opening'] = display_df['Opening'].round(2)
display_df['Closing'] = display_df['Closing'].round(2)
display_df['High'] = display_df['High'].round(2)
display_df['Low'] = display_df['Low'].round(2)
display_df['Change'] = display_df['Change'].round(2)
display_df['Change %'] = display_df['Change %'].round(2)
display_df['Avg Volume'] = display_df['Avg Volume'].round(0).astype(int)
display_df['Trading Days'] = display_df['Trading Days'].astype(int)

# Create the GT table
gt = GT(display_df)

# Format columns
gt = (gt
      .fmt_number(columns=['Opening', 'Closing', 'High', 'Low', 'Change'], decimals=2)
      .fmt_number(columns=['Change %'], decimals=2)
      .fmt_integer(columns=['Avg Volume', 'Trading Days'])
)

# Format the Change % column with proper suffix
gt = gt.sub_missing(missing_text='—')

# Add title and subtitle
gt = (gt
      .tab_header(
          title="S&P 500: 5-Year Performance Overview",
          subtitle=f"Daily data from {start_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')} to {end_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')}"
      )
)

# Style the header
gt = (gt
      .tab_style(
          style=style(
              background_color="#1f77b4",
              color="white",
              font_weight="bold",
              font_size="16px"
          ),
          locations="title"
      )
      .tab_style(
          style=style(
              color="#555555",
              font_size="13px"
          ),
          locations="subtitle"
      )
)

# Add column labels with proper styling
gt = gt.tab_spanner(
    label="Prices",
    columns=['Opening', 'Closing', 'High', 'Low']
)

gt = gt.tab_spanner(
    label="Year Performance",
    columns=['Change', 'Change %']
)

gt = gt.tab_spanner(
    label="Trading Activity",
    columns=['Avg Volume', 'Trading Days']
)

# Color code the Change % column
def color_change(val):
    if isinstance(val, str) or pd.isna(val):
        return 'background-color: #f0f0f0'
    if val > 0:
        return 'background-color: #d4edda; color: #155724; font-weight: bold'  # Green for positive
    elif val < 0:
        return 'background-color: #f8d7da; color: #721c24; font-weight: bold'  # Red for negative
    else:
        return 'background-color: #e2e3e5'  # Gray for zero

# Apply conditional formatting to Change % column
for i in range(len(display_df)):
    if display_df.loc[i, 'Year'] != '5-Year Overview':
        val = display_df.loc[i, 'Change %']
        color = color_change(val)

# Add styling for better readability
gt = (gt
      .tab_style(
          style=style(
              background_color="#f8f9fa",
              font_weight="bold"
          ),
          locations="body",
          rows=0  # First row (Overview)
      )
)

# Color the Change column and Change % column based on positive/negative
for i in range(1, len(display_df)):
    change_val = display_df.loc[i, 'Change %']
    if change_val > 0:
        gt = gt.tab_style(
            style=style(
                background_color="#d4edda",
                color="#155724",
                font_weight="bold"
            ),
            locations=f"body",
            rows=i,
            cols=['Change', 'Change %']
        )
    elif change_val < 0:
        gt = gt.tab_style(
            style=style(
                background_color="#f8d7da",
                color="#721c24",
                font_weight="bold"
            ),
            locations=f"body",
            rows=i,
            cols=['Change', 'Change %']
        )

# Format column headers with better styling
gt = (gt
      .tab_style(
          style=style(
              background_color="#e9ecef",
              font_weight="bold",
              text_align="center"
          ),
          locations="column_labels"
      )
)

# Add column spanning header styling
gt = (gt
      .tab_style(
          style=style(
              background_color="#dee2e6",
              font_weight="bold",
              text_align="center"
          ),
          locations="column_spanners"
      )
)

# Add summary footer row with 5-year totals
gt = gt.summary_rows(
    columns=['Trading Days'],
    fns='sum',
    formatter=lambda x: f"Total: {int(x):,}" if x > 0 else "—"
)

# Save as PNG
gt.save('table.png', scale=1.5)

print("✓ Table created successfully: table.png")
print(f"\nS&P 500 Summary (5-Year Overview):")
print(f"Start: {start_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')} at {overall_first_close:.2f}")
print(f"End: {end_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')} at {overall_last_close:.2f}")
print(f"5-Year Change: {overall_change:+.2f} ({overall_pct_change:+.2f}%)")
print(f"5-Year High: {overall_high:.2f}")
print(f"5-Year Low: {overall_low:.2f}")

end_date = df['date'].max()

    last_close = year_data.iloc[-1]['close']

    # Calculate the change and percentage change
    year_change = last_close - first_close
    year_pct_change = (year_change / first_close) * 100

    # Get high and low for the year
    year_high = year_data['high'].max()
    year_low = year_data['low'].min()

    # Get opening price for the year
    opening = year_data.iloc[0]['open']

    # Get total volume for the year
    total_volume = year_data['volume'].sum()

    yearly_stats.append({
        'Year': year,
        'Opening': opening,
        'Closing': last_close,
        'High': year_high,
        'Low': year_low,
        'Change': year_change,
        'Change %': year_pct_change,
        'Avg Volume': total_volume / len(year_data),
        'Trading Days': len(year_data)
    })

# Create DataFrame from yearly stats
summary_df = pd.DataFrame(yearly_stats)

# Calculate overall 5-year statistics
overall_first_close = df.iloc[0]['close']

overall_low = df['low'].min()

    'High': overall_high,
    'Low': overall_low,
    'Change': overall_change,
    'Change %': overall_pct_change,

    change_val = display_df.loc[i, 'Change %']
    if change_val > 0:
        # Green for positive
        gt = gt.tab_style(
            style=[
                fill(color="#d4edda"),
                text(color="#155724", weight="bold")
            ],
            locations=loc.body(rows=[i], columns=['Change', 'Change %'])
        )
    elif change_val < 0:
        # Red for negative
        gt = gt.tab_style(
            style=[
                fill(color="#f8d7da"),
                text(color="#721c24", weight="bold")
            ],
            locations=loc.body(rows=[i], columns=['Change', 'Change %'])
        )

# Save as PNG using gtsave (newer approach)
gt.gtsave('table.png')

print("✓ Table created successfully: table.png")
print(f"\nS&P 500 Summary (5-Year Overview):")
print(f"Period: {start_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')} to {end_date.strftime('%B %d, %Y')}")
print(f"Start: {overall_first_close:.2f}")
print(f"End: {overall_last_close:.2f}")
print(f"5-Year Change: {overall_change:+.2f} ({overall_pct_change:+.2f}%)")
print(f"5-Year High: {overall_high:.2f}")
print(f"5-Year Low: {overall_low:.2f}")
print(f"\nYearly Breakdown:")
for _, row in summary_df.iterrows():
    print(f"  {int(row['Year'])}: {row['Change %']:+.2f}%")
